title: Notes from the field.
kicker: Short pieces on operating model, attribution, and where reporting actually breaks. Three or four a year — only when there's something a CFO would actually use.

items.title: RAS, not ROAS — stop reporting the platform's number.
items.excerpt: Meta and Google count revenue against the wrong denominator. Here's the substitute I run weekly with three of my retainers, and why the gap to the CFO's report shrunk from 38% to under 4% in two quarters.

items.title: The two-week diagnostic: what to demand before any retainer.
items.excerpt: Every account I've inherited was overspending on something it couldn't defend. A short list of the tests I run in week one, the questions I ask before I accept the brief, and the one chart I refuse to ship without.

items.title: An Arabic-first creative stack that doesn't read as translated.
items.excerpt: Most regional accounts I audit are running hand-rendered Arabic on top of English-led concepts. That choice quietly halves their CTR. The fix is structural, not stylistic — and the operating model behind it took two years to get right.
